12.reason: [The expression ~(c = fgetc(IN)) is incorrect for checking EOF. fgetc returns an int, not a char, and ~ operator causes undefined behavior when applied to char. Also, EOF is negative, so bitwise NOT will not yield correct loop termination. Should use (c = fgetc(IN)) != EOF with c declared as int.]

17.code: while (s != l && strcmp(s->s, str) < 0) s = s->ne;
17.reason: [This assumes the list is sorted, but insertion logic does not maintain lexicographical order correctly due to flawed comparison and linking. Also, initial node l[0] is uninitialized, leading to undefined behavior in strcmp(s->s, str).]

20.reason: [This syntax (compound literal assignment to array element) may not be supported in older C standards (e.g., C89), and even in C99+, the initialization of struct with designated initializer inside array assignment is invalid here. 33.code: for (string *i = head; i != l; i = i->ne)
33.reason: [Loop condition assumes l is a sentinel, but l[0] was never properly initialized as end marker, and the list may not terminate at l, leading to infinite loop or crash. Also, if no words were read, head == l and loop doesn't run, which is okay, but generally unsafe.]
